page.title: Classify the past, then see how it still shapes the present
page.description: Five dimensions organize, filter, and review memory. Formative power stays attached to individual memories and growth nodes instead of becoming a primary filter.

type.title: Type
type.description: Start with the form this memory is carried by.

era.title: Era
era.description: Eras can be multi-select. Primary era is only for timeline placement.

emotionalWeight.title: Emotional Weight
emotionalWeight.description: How heavy does it feel to touch now?

processing.title: Processing
processing.description: Not every part of the past needs to be fully processed.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Sensitive memories need clear boundaries before review.

classification.title: 5-D Overview
classification.description: These dimensions belong in filtering and grouping. Formative power stays on the timeline and in detail.

formativePower.title: Formative Power
formativePower.description: Formative power explains depth of influence and is not a primary filter.

timeline.title: Memory Timeline
timeline.description: Review memories by primary era while always keeping formative power visible.

growth.title: Growth Nodes
growth.description: When multiple memories point in the same direction of change, they form a growth node.

anchors.title: Sensory Anchors
anchors.description: Many memories do not start with events. They begin with places, objects, or photos.

reviewPrompts.title: Review Prompts
reviewPrompts.description: These prompts help you approach the past gradually instead of forcing a full retelling.

threads.title: Influences Still Active Now
threads.description: Signals surfacing from growth nodes that are worth cross-checking with beliefs, principles, and relationships.

control.title: Structure Review
control.description: Control mode can maintain memories, growth nodes, anchors, prompts, and active signals while also spotting missing fields and broken links.
control.missingLinksTitle: Memory IDs referenced by growth nodes but not found in the current dataset

edit.listHint: One per line, or separate with commas.
edit.sourceModulesHint: Only recognized source module names are saved.
edit.memoryIdsHint: One ID per line, or separate with commas; invalid IDs are ignored.
edit.memoryDescription: Maintain a memory's classification, summary, impact, and review signals.
edit.growthDescription: Use memory IDs to connect before, after, and trigger memories.
edit.anchorDescription: Connect places, objects, people, or photos with memories.
edit.promptDescription: Record a question that can be revisited slowly.
edit.threadDescription: Record an influence that is still active and worth reviewing across modules.

validation.memoryRequired: Enter a title, primary era, summary, and impact
validation.growthRequired: Enter title, before/after states, key event, and a valid trigger memory
validation.anchorRequired: Enter the anchor name and note

empty.memories: No memories match the current filters.
empty.growthNodes: No growth nodes match the current filters.
empty.anchors: No memory anchors match the current filters.
empty.reviewPrompts: No review prompts match the current filters.
empty.threads: No active influence signals match the current filters.
empty.signals: No supporting signals match the current filters.
